title: Separate the Duties of Individuals to Reduce the Risk of Malevolent Activity
description: separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion

checks.name: Duties Requiring Separation are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that distinct roles with separated duties are defined via local group membership and Group Policy, ensuring no single user holds conflicting privileges

checks.name: Responsibilities for Separated Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Confirm that no single user account is a member of both administrative and standard user groups simultaneously, enforcing separation of responsibilities

checks.name: Access Privileges Enabling Individual Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Verify that access privileges are scoped per role and no individual holds excessive cross-role privileges that would undermine separation of duties

checks.name: Duties Requiring Separation are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that distinct roles with separated duties are defined via AD security groups and Group Policy, ensuring no single account holds conflicting administrative privileges

checks.name: Responsibilities for Separated Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Confirm that no single AD user account is a member of multiple high-privilege groups simultaneously such as Domain Admins and Backup Operators

checks.name: Access Privileges Enabling Individual Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Verify that access privileges are scoped per role via AD group assignments and no individual holds excessive cross-role privileges that would undermine separation of duties

checks.name: Duties Requiring Separation are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that distinct roles with separated duties are defined via group membership, sudoers scoping, and PAM controls with no single user holding conflicting privileges

checks.name: Responsibilities for Separated Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Confirm that no single user account is a member of both sudo/wheel and other sensitive groups simultaneously, enforcing separation of responsibilities

checks.name: Access Privileges Enabling Individual Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Verify that service accounts and user accounts are kept separate and no individual user account is also used as a service identity

checks.name: Duties Requiring Separation are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that distinct roles with separated duties are defined via group membership, sudoers scoping, and PAM controls with no single user holding conflicting privileges

checks.name: Responsibilities for Separated Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Confirm that no single user account is a member of both sudo/wheel and other sensitive groups simultaneously, enforcing separation of responsibilities

checks.name: Access Privileges Enabling Individual Duties are Assigned to Separate Individuals
checks.purpose: Verify that service accounts and user accounts are kept separate and no individual user account is also used as a service identity

remediation: Identify all privileged functions and sensitive operations and document which roles are responsible for each. Ensure no single user account holds conflicting privileges that would allow them to complete a sensitive transaction without a second party. On Windows, verify that no account is simultaneously a member of administrative and standard user groups in a way that undermines role separation. On Linux, ensure no user is a member of both the sudo or wheel group and other sensitive groups simultaneously. Review and recertify role assignments at least annually.
